title: Communications Equipment Maintenance
entry_mechanism: Saudi electronics maintenance workshops upgraded to OEM-certified maintenance capability
executive_summary:
Military tactical radio and communications systems — SINCGARS, PRC-117G, Harris RF-7800, and Rohde & Schwarz MBITR equivalents across Saudi ground forces — require periodic maintenance, calibration, and component-level repair that today flows almost entirely to foreign OEM service centres. The maintenance requirement is large: Saudi Arabia operates hundreds of thousands of tactical communications devices across all service branches.

Communications equipment maintenance is the most accessible electronics service entry point in the defence sector because: the equipment is less classified than fire control or radar systems; OEM maintenance certification programmes are well-established internationally; the test equipment is commercially available; and Saudi Arabia's existing commercial communications maintenance industry provides an upgrade baseline.

Node DE-3 targets OEM-certified Saudi maintenance workshops capable of depot-level repair for the priority communications equipment categories — primarily Rohde & Schwarz (German, non-ITAR) and Harris/L3Harris (US, ITAR-controlled — limited scope) systems. Phase I focus on non-ITAR European-origin systems, Phase II expansion to ITAR categories under bilateral agreement.

companies.name: Rohde & Schwarz
companies.rationale: R&S is the primary supplier of non-ITAR tactical radios and communications to Saudi Arabia's forces. R&S has a structured Authorised Maintenance Centre programme that Saudi workshops can qualify for.
companies.contact: R&S VP Government and Defence Customer Support, Middle East

companies.name: Thales Communications
companies.rationale: Thales supplies tactical communications to Saudi Arabia and has maintenance certification programmes for allied nations.
companies.contact: Thales Communications VP After-Sales, Saudi Arabia

companies.name: Leonardo Communications
companies.rationale: Leonardo supplies communications equipment to Gulf defence forces and has Italian government support for maintenance localisation.
companies.contact: Leonardo VP Communications After-Sales, Middle East

companies.name: Harris / L3Harris
companies.rationale: L3Harris supplies SINCGARS and PRC-117G to Saudi Arabia. Maintenance in-Kingdom requires US government authorisation — engage through the US DSCA bilateral agreement process.

companies.name: Frequentis
companies.rationale: Frequentis supplies military communication management systems and has active Gulf engagement. Non-ITAR Austrian technology.

partners.name: SAMI Electronics / Communications Division
partners.current: Some communications maintenance contracts; basic radio servicing
partners.upgrade: Establish R&S Authorised Maintenance Centre; invest in R&S test equipment suite; hire 30 Saudi communications technicians; expand to Thales and Leonardo maintenance
partners.anchor: SAMI + Saudi Army + Royal Saudi Air Force

partners.name: Saudi Telecom / STC
partners.current: Large-scale communications infrastructure maintenance; extensive RF and microwave capability
partners.upgrade: Defence communications maintenance division; military communications security protocols; R&S certification programme

partners.name: National Communication Company (NCC)
partners.current: Communications maintenance and installation for Saudi government
partners.upgrade: Military communications maintenance qualification; R&S and Thales certification; NSCS clearance for classified system categories

partners.name: TVTC Electronics Training
partners.upgrade: Military communications maintenance specialisation; R&S factory training pathway; Saudi military technician certification programme
